meta.title: FanOps canonical ground-truth inventory
meta.method: direct read + grep of src/fanops at working-tree HEAD. Docs used as leads only, never as proof.
meta.evidence_standard: every item carries >=1 {file,line} citation into source. Items without one are typed 'unknown' and listed under unknowns[].
meta.id_stability: IDs are permanent. Never renumber. New items append. Retired items keep their ID with status='retired'.

extensions.rationale: Cycle-1 required fingerprints. A fingerprint is a derivation RULE, not a cache; folding it into 'cache' would lose the hashed-input list, which is the load-bearing fact.
Cycle-1 required filesystem locations. A pipeline stage directory is not an artifact (it holds them) nor a datastore.
Concurrency primitives are load-bearing and have no home in the 12. Not a 'worker'.
ID-minting functions determine every entity key. Distinct from fingerprint (which addresses content, not identity).

completeness.uninspected_note: studio/app_routes_*.py are counted as structurally scanned for their ROUTE SURFACE only (decorators extracted); their handler bodies are unread.

items.name: launchd agent: main run daemon

concurrent source pre-warm pool
items.notes: map-parallel / reduce-serial; the single main transaction remains the only ledger writer

items.name: ledger write lock (fcntl.flock)
items.rationale: kernel RELEASES an flock when the holder dies, so a killed writer self-heals (chosen over an O_EXCL sentinel)

items.name: stage lock
items.notes: mirrors ledger._file_lock; self-heals an orphaned lockfile

items.name: run lease (.run.lock)
items.mechanism: fcntl.flock LOCK_NB; lockfile body (pid + started ISO) is ADVISORY for status only

items.name: configuration precedence

00_control/tuning.json (SEPARATE narrow channel: HOLD-gate regexes + lift weights only)
items.settings_vs_config: UNPROVEN. Settings (settings.py:141, a pydantic BaseSettings) and Config (direct os.getenv) BOTH read the same env keys. Which wins where, and whether they can disagree, is not established.

environment_variables.note: 63 FANOPS_* + 12 external keys are read by src/. Defaults cited from the read site. FANOPS_CFG is EXCLUDED - it is a Flask app.config key (studio/app.py:274), NOT an env var.

taxonomy_changelog.reason: SHIM-007 (RenderState.{queued,published,analyzed,retired}) was filed under historical_compatibility_shims while its OWN verdict field read 'RESERVED SURFACE - not dead config, not a shim'. A category must follow the item's description, never its ID prefix. A reserved surface is a declared-but-unwired lifecycle: not dead, not a shim, not a fixture.
taxonomy_changelog.id_policy: SHIM-007 RETAINS its permanent ID. Per meta.id_stability, IDs are never renumbered - only the category and an explicit `classification` field changed. Cycles 2-7 may continue to cite SHIM-007 unambiguously.

taxonomy_changelog.reason: The parent key asserted DEADNESS over all its children, but only 1 of its 5 sub-buckets (confirmed_dead, 5 items) is actually dead. The other 13 of 18 items - phantoms, test fixtures, compat shims, reserved surfaces - are NOT dead. A machine consumer reading dead_configuration.* would have mis-typed 13 items. This is the same class of error as TXC-001, one level up.
taxonomy_changelog.consumers_at_time_of_change: none (Cycle 1 closed the same day this file was created)

taxonomy_changelog.change: RENAMED: historical_compatibility_shims -> compatibility_shims; added per-item `direction` field
taxonomy_changelog.reason: SHIM-005 (pydantic extra='ignore') is FORWARD-compat - an OLDER binary parsing a NEWER ledger. 'Historical' is false for it. It IS a compatibility shim, so it stays in the bucket; the adjective was the wrong part. `direction` (backward|forward) now carries the fact the category name was overloading. Exactly 1 of 6 is forward, which is why the misnomer went unnoticed.

configuration_classification.note: Item 5 of the Cycle-2 brief. Dead config, phantoms, test fixtures, compatibility shims, and reserved surfaces are FIVE DIFFERENT THINGS. Only `confirmed_dead` is dead. Parent key renamed from 'dead_configuration' (TXC-002) because it asserted deadness over 13 items that are alive.

confirmed_dead.name: FANOPS_CASTING_BIAS
confirmed_dead.reason: the P11 LLM-casting stage (moment_casting, durable AccountSelection, casting_bias) was REMOVED. Ledger migration v11 (_migrate_v10_drop_selections, ledger.py:229) is the teardown's on-disk half.
confirmed_dead.verdict: DEAD - a removed feature's flag, still named in tests

confirmed_dead.name: FANOPS_CREATIVE_VARIATION
confirmed_dead.reason: the per-account render FORK (variant_hook, per-handle hook maps) was DELETED; one owner-moment hook now burns at crosspost

confirmed_dead.name: FANOPS_HOOK_EDITOR
confirmed_dead.reason: the hook editor/critic/taxonomy was DELETED; is_weak_hook is the sole gate

confirmed_dead.name: 00_control/ledger.lock
confirmed_dead.reason: declared and labelled VESTIGIAL in code; accounts/personas use their own flocks
confirmed_dead.verdict: DEAD - a declared path with no live consumer

phantom_never_existed.name: FANOPS_SKIP_PREPUSH
phantom_never_existed.verdict: NOT A CONFIG VAR AT ALL

evidence.text: There is deliberately NO FANOPS_SKIP_PREPUSH / test-skip bypass

phantom_never_existed.reason: it exists ONLY in prose asserting its non-existence, plus a test PROVING its absence. Cycle 1 wrongly bucketed it as a 'test/CI-only env var', implying it was a real knob.

test_fixtures_not_product_config.name: FANOPS_REQUIRE_E2E
test_fixtures_not_product_config.purpose: CI gate: turns an integration SKIP into a FAIL

compatibility_shims.name: FANOPS_POSTER (the legacy global poster bridge)
compatibility_shims.reason: per-channel routing in accounts.json is the truth (Accounts.effective_provider); FANOPS_POSTER only narrows the back-compat fallback for a provider-less channel. go_live NEVER writes it.
compatibility_shims.verdict: COMPAT SHIM - deliberately retained, not dead

compatibility_shims.name: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
compatibility_shims.reason: the responder uses the operator's `claude login` session. Kept harmless for a third-party/Bedrock setup that exports it.

compatibility_shims.name: HookSource.per_account
compatibility_shims.status: legacy provenance label retained for OLD Render rows (pre-P9 per-handle map)
compatibility_shims.verdict: COMPAT SHIM - enum member kept for deserialization

compatibility_shims.direction_note: THE reason TXC-003 renamed this category: an OLDER binary parsing a NEWER ledger is not 'historical' compat. Exactly 1 of 6 shims is forward, which is why the misnomer survived the first write.
compatibility_shims.name: pydantic extra='ignore' on every ledger model
compatibility_shims.reason: an OLDER binary loading a ledger written by a NEWER schema must parse it, dropping unknown keys, never crash. Switching any ledger model to extra='forbid' would turn a forward-rolled ledger into a hard ControlFileError.

compatibility_shims.name: legacy StartInterval plists
compatibility_shims.status: still round-trip; current installs use KeepAlive + env-carried interval

reserved_surfaces.id_note: ID RETAINED (TXC-001). The 'SHIM-' prefix is now a historical artifact of this item's original mis-filing, NOT a claim about its type. Per meta.id_stability, IDs are permanent and are never renumbered when a classification is corrected. Cite SHIM-007 with classification='reserved_surface'.
reserved_surfaces.name: RenderState.{queued,published,analyzed,retired}
reserved_surfaces.definition: A lifecycle that exists in the type system with no code path that advances it. Distinct from dead config (which a removed feature left behind), from a compat shim (which serves an older or newer peer), and from a test fixture (which serves CI).
reserved_surfaces.reason: No code writes any RenderState value other than the birth default 'rendered' - verified exhaustively across deserialization, serialization, migrations, the ledger API, Studio actions, reconciliation, and publishing. See FIND-001.
reserved_surfaces.why_the_members_are_kept: views_results._SHIPPABLE_RENDER (views_results.py:112) reads the enum BY NAME. The members cannot be dropped without changing that reader.
reserved_surfaces.live_consequence: Because a Render is always 'rendered', the _SHIPPABLE_RENDER guard (rendered|queued|published|analyzed) is ALWAYS satisfied and is therefore a no-op. Only 'retired' would gate it, and nothing sets 'retired'.
reserved_surfaces.not_dead_because: a driver could be wired without changing the type; no feature was removed that used to drive it
reserved_surfaces.not_a_shim_because: it serves no older or newer peer, no legacy on-disk shape, and no external contract

findings.title: RenderState has no driver - CONFIRMED across all five surfaces, with two refinements
findings.claim_under_test: Cycle 1 asserted 'RenderState has no driver' on the strength of a code COMMENT (models.py:93-101). Cycle 2 was asked to verify it independently.
findings.method: exhaustive grep for RenderState.* references, Render( construction sites, and '.state =' assignments on render variables across all 127 modules, then targeted reads of each surface.

deserialization.result: state IS parsed from the stored row
deserialization.detail: Render(**v) - so a persisted non-'rendered' state WOULD round-trip back into memory

migrations.detail: v6 is a pure additive injection: {**raw, 'renders': raw.get('renders', {})}

ledger_api.detail: add_render is setdefault (first-write-wins); get_render is a read. A renders_of_account helper was REMOVED for having zero callers (ledger.py:620).

publishing.result: the renders MAP is mutated - but state is NOT
publishing.detail: led.renders[post.render_id] = r.model_copy(update={'path': str(shrunk)}) - a path rewrite after compression. model_copy carries every other field through unchanged, INCLUDING state.

refinements: REFINEMENT 1 (missed by Cycle 1): the renders map IS written on the publish path (post/compress.py:112 and :130), so 'Renders are never mutated' would be FALSE. Only Render.state is never mutated.

REFINEMENT 2: the invariant is 'no CODE writes RenderState', NOT 'the field cannot hold another value'. Deserialization (ledger.py:458) faithfully restores whatever state is on disk, so a hand-edited or externally-written ledger row could carry a non-'rendered' state and it would load and persist.
findings.consequence: Only two RenderState references exist outside the model default: the birth default (models.py:420) and the _SHIPPABLE_RENDER read (views_results.py:112). Since a Render is always 'rendered' in practice, the _SHIPPABLE_RENDER guard (rendered|queued|published|analyzed) is ALWAYS satisfied and is therefore a no-op. Only 'retired' would gate, and nothing sets it.
findings.status: SETTLED - safe to treat as ground truth for Cycles 3-7

findings.title: The 'single writer of queued' invariant in src/fanops/CLAUDE.md is FALSE as written; the safety property it protects nevertheless HOLDS
findings.claim_under_test: src/fanops/CLAUDE.md: 'do NOT set a post's state to queued anywhere except Ledger.approve_post (ledger.py:503, promotes at :519)'
findings.why_safety_holds: EVERY non-approve_post writer of 'queued' is guarded on a SOURCE state of failed, error, or submitting. None of them can move a post out of awaiting_approval. So 'an unapproved post is structurally unpublishable' survives intact - it is approve_post's guard at ledger.py:579 that enforces it, not a single-writer property.
findings.secondary: CLAUDE.md's line references are STALE: it cites ledger.py:503/:519 for approve_post; the real location is ledger.py:575 (guard at :579). ledger.py:503-519 is now _save_unlocked/save.

findings.title: studio_audit.log ownership RESOLVED - it is the one control file whose path is built inline rather than declared in Config

writer.mode: append, one JSON line per state-changing action

findings.structural_note: Every other control file is declared as a Config attribute (config.py:157-177). This one is constructed inline at BOTH the write and read site (`cfg.control / "studio_audit.log"`), duplicating the literal. Recorded as a fact; no recommendation per the fact-only brief.

findings.title: A SECOND undeclared control file: the run lease .run.lock
findings.detail: _LOCK_NAME = '.run.lock'. Like studio_audit.log (FIND-003), it lives under 00_control but is NOT declared in Config.__init__. fcntl.flock LOCK_NB is the authority; the lockfile BODY (pid + started ISO) is advisory for status/diagnostics only. Kernel releases on process death, so kill -9 self-heals.
findings.guards: the respond->write_request->advance converge loop (mutual exclusion per workspace)

findings.title: Cycle-1 error: the framing tunables are in clip.py, not framing.py
findings.detail: Cycle 1 marked _FACE_FRAC_TALK, _FACE_FRAC_MUSIC, _EYELINE_FRAC, _ZOOM_MAX, _ZOOM_MAX_TRACK, _ZOOM_MAX_FAR, _SMALL_FACE_FRAC, _TARGETS and _REFRAME_GEOM_V as UNKNOWN because it grepped framing.py. They are all in clip.py. _REFRAME_GEOM_V = 4 (clip.py:616), matching the CLAUDE.md claim.

findings.title: Cycle-1 error: FANOPS_SKIP_PREPUSH is a phantom, not a test-only env var
findings.detail: Cycle 1 listed it under 'test/CI-only env vars', implying a real knob. It is the opposite: the pre-push hook explicitly documents that NO such bypass exists (.githooks/pre-push:8) and a test ASSERTS its absence (tests/test_check_scripts.py:239).

findings.title: Search blind-spot: constants expressed as keyword-argument defaults are invisible to constant-shaped greps
findings.detail: The hashtag cap (max_tags: int = 4, hashtags.py:232) was nearly recorded as unproven because greps for MAX_HASHTAGS / [:4] / _CAP returned nothing. The absence was an artifact of the search pattern, not of the code. Any future cycle enumerating 'tunable constants' must also scan default parameter values, not just module-level assignments.

unknowns.subject: Persona identity field + minting function
unknowns.priority: high - Persona is a first-class entity and Account.persona_id joins to it

unknowns.subject: intro_match gate-key -> source resolution
unknowns.detail: gate_keys.gate_source_id (gate_keys.py:9-13) branches on 'moments'/'moment_hooks' and falls through to a CLIP-keyed lookup for everything else. An intro_match gate exists (models.py:653-667, config.py:725-732) but has no branch. Whether it is clip-keyed by design or is an unhandled kind is UNPROVEN.
unknowns.priority: high - a mis-resolved gate key silently orphans a gate

unknowns.subject: TikTok oEmbed caller
unknowns.detail: the URL literal https://www.tiktok.com/oembed exists in src/ but the calling module and purpose were not located

unknowns.subject: 00_control/context.md reader/writer
unknowns.detail: declared in Config; no consumer located

unknowns.subject: VETTED frozen-floor cardinality
unknowns.detail: VETTED = set(niche_floor('rap')) | set(niche_floor('gossip')) | set(_ARABIC) | ... (hashtags.py:52). The pool GENRES are known (rap, gossip) and _ARABIC has 3 members, but the total count was not computed.

unknowns.subject: post/__init__.py:19 get_poster live-guard
unknowns.detail: src/fanops/CLAUDE.md claims get_poster RAISES rather than build a DryRunPoster when live. This is one half of the two-gate dryrun/live invariant. NOT independently verified against code this cycle - post/__init__.py is uninspected.
unknowns.priority: HIGH - this is a publish-safety invariant

unknowns.subject: Settings vs Config precedence
unknowns.detail: settings.py:141 (pydantic BaseSettings) and config.py (direct os.getenv) BOTH read the same env keys. settings.py carries its own validators (_validate_poster, _validate_responder, _strict_validate_*). Which layer wins at which callsite, and whether they can disagree, is UNPROVEN.
unknowns.priority: HIGH - two config readers that can disagree is a correctness hazard

unknowns.subject: Clip.meta_captions key format
unknowns.detail: an untyped dict keyed by 'surface' (models.py:288). surface_key() yields '{account}|{platform}' (ids.py:23), but that the dict actually uses this shape was not proven at the WRITE site.

unknowns.subject: Studio per-route handler/action/view ownership
unknowns.detail: 149 route decorators across 8 files were enumerated by path, but no route was traced to its handler, its action, or its ledger effect.
unknowns.priority: HIGH - the Studio is the operator's primary mutation surface

unknowns.subject: _DAEMON_TRANSIENT_MAX value
unknowns.detail: referenced at post/run.py:336 and :405 (bounds the daemon requeue loop) but the literal was not read

operational_findings.note: Item 8 of the Cycle-2 brief: these are facts about the WORKING TREE / operator environment, NOT about the architecture. They must not be read as codebase properties.

items.title: A stale orchestration wave marker is engaged
items.effect: The gate hook denies every subagent spawn outside {fanops-worker, fanops-lander} and refuses any shell command naming .orchestration/state/, .cursor/hooks*, or .githooks/ - including read-only ls/cat.
items.consequence_for_this_work: Cycles 1 and 2 were executed SINGLE-THREADED. No parallel subsystem tracing was possible.
items.disengage_path: orchestrate.py done (measured, auto-disengages on a pristine sweep) or orchestrate.py stop (OPERATOR-ONLY, denied from inside a run)

items.title: Untracked build/ directory and git worktrees present in the tree
items.effect: The worktrees contain FULL repo copies. A naive repo-wide grep double-counts every hit (this was observed while resolving PHANTOM-001). Any future cycle must scope greps to src/, tests/, docs/ and exclude .claude/worktrees/.

cycle2.title: Architecture, Mutation & Invariant Verification
cycle2.note: APPEND-ONLY. Cycle 1 sections stand except where corrections[] says otherwise. IDs are never renumbered.

documents.MUTATION_MATRIX.md: every mutable field + full concurrency audit
documents.COUPLINGS.md: 15 proven hidden couplings + all 149 Studio routes attributed

unknowns_resolved.verdict: no precedence exists; Settings never feeds Config
Persona has no state field and no minted id; key is the personas.json record key

corrections.why: one live consumer: Ledger.restore_snapshot takes fcntl.flock on 00_control/ledger.lock while EVERY other ledger writer serializes on SQLite BEGIN IMMEDIATE. The two locks are mutually invisible; restore then os.replace()s the db file.
corrections.exposure: nil today - restore_snapshot has no production caller
corrections.meta: Cycle 1 classified this on the strength of a code comment (config.py:159 'vestigial'), violating its own method note #2.

corrections.consequence: ledger._LIVE_CLIP_STATES is a DEAD GUARD - clip_live is always False at ledger.py:665; cascade protection rests entirely on _PROTECTED_POST_STATES

corrections.refinement: the two Render writes on the publish path are now pinned: run.py:363 (media_url) and run.py:367 (path). Render.state remains unwritten.

findings.summary: get_poster live-guard raises only on the literal string 'dryrun'; an UNRECOGNIZED backend falls through to DryRunPoster on a LIVE system. Guard is case-insensitive, PROVIDERS lookup is case-sensitive.
findings.blast_radius: post stranded in `submitting` (NOT a phantom published row - DryRunPoster sets no state post-M2); escalated to needs_reconcile at 72h then GAVE UP:

findings.summary: Settings is never constructed by Config. Settings.runtime_load has ZERO callers. Config reads os.getenv directly (74 calls, uncached). No runtime precedence conflict because there is no runtime handoff.
intro_match is live-wired (pipeline.py:242-244) but absent from responder._SCHEMA/_PROMPT AND gate_keys.gate_source_id - the responder can never answer it.

R1 published-URL invariant enforced at CONSTRUCTION only. Proven vs pydantic 2.13.4: model_copy + setattr bypass it, the bad row serializes, and the NEXT Ledger.load raises -> ControlFileError -> whole ledger unloadable. Two code comments claim 'Pydantic re-validates on serialization' - FALSE.
findings.property_holds_via: four independent MANUAL call-site guards (run.py:305, actions.py:268, cli.py:384, dryrun.py:39)

findings.summary: Seven more never-written state-enum members; RenderState was not a one-off. Ledger.set_post_state has zero callers.
149 Studio routes: 108 mutating, 41 GET, ZERO authenticated (no auth/session/CSRF/before_request). Security boundary is the network interface only.
Every CLAUDE.md safety-claim line reference is stale (10 checked, 10 wrong). Names and semantics correct; citations rotted.

operational.OPS-001: STILL ENGAGED - an Explore subagent spawn was REFUSED by the orchestration gate during this cycle. Cycle 2 ran single-threaded.

cycle3.title: Runtime execution, failure propagation, recovery, side-effect boundaries
cycle3.method: AST census over all 127 modules (exception handlers, network/subprocess/env/txn/retry sites) + full manual read of the publish, reconcile, gate, daemon and recovery paths + 11 CONTROLLED EXECUTION EXPERIMENTS against temporary FanOps roots with fake providers and an injected clock. NO live ledger, NO network, NO destructive command was run.
cycle3.authority_note: Where Cycle-2 JSON and executable code disagreed, code won and the contradiction is recorded in CYCLE3_CORRECTIONS.md by stable ID. Historical findings are NOT rewritten.

corrections_to_prior_cycles.was: submitting -> needs_reconcile escalates at 72h; total to GAVE UP = 72h + 72h
corrections_to_prior_cycles.now: escalation is at 24h (_SUBMITTING_ESCALATE_AFTER, reconcile.py:48). Cycle 2 cited reconcile.py:54, which is the DIFFERENT constant _RECONCILE_GIVEUP_AFTER (72h). Both ages are measured from scheduled_time (_parked_age), NOT chained. Total time-to-terminal is 72h, NOT 144h.

corrections_to_prior_cycles.was: the malformed-provider post is 'eventually labeled' (escalate then GAVE UP)
corrections_to_prior_cycles.now: TRUE on Postiz, FALSE on Zernio. The escalation and give-up sit on the SUCCESSFUL-POLL branch (reconcile.py:739); ZernioStatusClient RAISES on a 404 while PostizStatusClient returns {'status':'unknown'}. New finding C3-F2.

corrections_to_prior_cycles.was: _is_fake_token listed as part of the escalation guard, with no analysis of the inverse
corrections_to_prior_cycles.now: It is a GATE on the terminal path. A REAL submission_id is structurally excluded from BOTH terminals, on BOTH backends. New finding C3-F1. FALSIFIES brief claims #1 and #2.

It is rewritten INTO a tempfile.mkdtemp directory (compress.py:112 <- compress.py:21) and FINALIZE PERSISTS it. The ledger's durable canonical media pointer for a shrunk render is a temp path, and NOTHING cleans those dirs. New findings C3-F4 + C3-F5.

TWO live control-file writers bypass controlio and are NOT atomic: agentstep.bump_attempts (agentstep.py:147, bare write_text) and pipeline_run.note_stage (pipeline_run.py:64-69, ftruncate+write). FALSIFIES brief claim #8. New findings C3-F6 + C3-F7.

INV-11's PROPERTY (never burn the queue) holds for one pass. But the DAEMON re-drives a persistent AuthError every tick forever (cli.py:1311-1312), writes NO heartbeat, and changes no ledger state. New finding C3-F8.

daemon.status reports verdict `alive` while EVERY pass halts, because daemon_progress treats 'the newest run.log line of ANY kind' as liveness and a failing pass still writes stage lines. Misleading success. New finding C3-F9. Does NOT contradict project memory `liveness-verdict-single-owner` — it qualifies it.

INV-14 is TRUE as scoped (p4_dim_bias / variant_amplify / timing_bias) and is NOT retracted. But its scope hides the destructive path: adjust.retire — the ONLY IRREVERSIBLE learning actuator (adjust.py:95; reconcile_moments refuses to un-retire, ledger.py:636-642) — is gated on cfg.is_live_backend ONLY, not on learning_validated, and fires at n=3 analyzed posts. The reversible bias actuators wait for >=8. New finding C3-F10.

The orchestration gate refused an Explore subagent spawn AND denied the Write tool for a scratchpad script. THREE CONSECUTIVE CYCLES (1, 2, 3) have been executed single-threaded because a stale wave marker has never been disengaged. Operator action; clear before Cycle 4.

findings.title: A post with a REAL submission_id has NO terminal path in reconcile
The XC-1 escalation and XC-2 give-up are DEAD CODE on the Zernio backend
_requeue_transient_failed_for_daemon: the only log-free swallow in post/run.py
Unbounded temp-dir leak — fanops-shrink-* is never cleaned
Render.path is durably rewritten INTO a mkdtemp directory
A torn attempts.json silently resets the gate-retry ceiling
note_stage is a torn-write window on .run.lock
The daemon re-drives an unrecoverable AuthError every tick, forever, with no heartbeat
daemon.status reports `alive` while every pass halts
adjust.retire — the only IRREVERSIBLE learning actuator — has the WEAKEST gate
The F44 upload cache has no invalidation rule
framing._detect_faces returns PARTIAL results on an exception
A lost audit line is silent and unrecoverable (contract-correct)
The publish retry loop CANNOT double-POST — hypothesis tested and DISCONFIRMED
The requeue family cannot strand a real-sid post — hypothesis tested and DISCONFIRMED (all 5 clear submission_id). The strand arrives via bulk_send_to_review instead.

adversarial_checks.5_every_external_side_effect_is_idempote: UPHELD for the publish POST (claim-before-network + park-never-re-POST, both backends). PARTIAL for uploads (R2 content-addressed = yes; Postiz multipart = no).
adversarial_checks.6_every_dynamically_written_state_is_in_: UPHELD — the Cycle-2 AST census (transitions.json) stands; no new dynamic writer found
adversarial_checks.10_every_swallowed_exception_is_distingu: FALSIFIED (C3-F3, C3-F12); mostly upheld elsewhere — the house norm (fail-open WITH a logged breadcrumb) is real and CI-enforced by test_swallow_ratchet.py
adversarial_checks.11_every_background_task_has_an_observab: FALSIFIED (C3-F4 has NO signal of any kind; C3-F9 is a MISLEADING one)
adversarial_checks.12_every_atomic_claim_names_its_primitiv: FALSIFIED (C3-COR-05 — two writers claim the controlio contract and do not use it)

workflows_partially_traced: W8 (action-layer durability traced; the 108 mutating routes were NOT individually re-driven)
W10 (actuator gating + reversibility traced; meta_graph.py / variant_ucb / digest internals structurally scanned only)
completeness.honest_statement: Cycle 3 is NOT exhaustive. It is exhaustive for the PUBLISH, RECONCILE, GATE, DAEMON and RECOVERY paths — the ones that reach the network and the ones that strand. It is NOT exhaustive for the Studio view layer (38 broad handlers in views.py alone were counted but not individually traced) or for the Meta Graph / hashtag subsystem.

cycle4_inputs_prioritised: 1. C3-F1 + C3-F2 — the terminal-path hole. This is the single highest-value reconciliation target: two HIGH defects, one root (the escalation lives on the wrong branch and is gated on the wrong predicate).
2. Cycle-2 F-A (malformed provider -> DryRunPoster when live) — now compounded by C3-F2, because on Zernio the resulting strand is never labeled AT ALL.
3. C3-F4 + C3-F5 — the shrink temp leak and the durable temp pointer. These are COUPLED: naively fixing the leak would dangle every shrunk render's path.
4. C3-F9 + C3-F8 — the daemon liveness verdict conflates 'process running' with 'work succeeding'.
5. INV-01 (the load-time poison pill) — still latent, still the highest-leverage invariant to enforce STRUCTURALLY rather than by four manual call-site guards.
6. C3-F10 — the irreversible learning actuator's gate.
7. OPS-001 — disengage the stale orchestration wave marker so Cycle 4 can parallelise.

cycle4.title: Root-cause defect model, remediation design, and change sequencing
cycle4.status: DESIGN ONLY — no production source or test was modified; no PR opened. The live ledger was read READ-ONLY (sqlite mode=ro) to settle one reachability question.

root_causes.RC-1: A decision that FORBIDS an action is evaluated AFTER the state change that AUTHORIZES it (run.py:272 claims; run.py:287 then skips). HIGH.
root_causes.RC-2: reconcile's terminal ladder is conditioned on poll success AND token provenance — neither is a property of 'is this post stuck?'. HIGH.
root_causes.RC-3: A backend id is normalized at ONE write boundary and consumed RAW by five resolvers with four unknown-value behaviours. HIGH.
root_causes.RC-3b: Reconcile is gated on is_live_backend; publish is not. The sole READER of `submitting` can be switched off while the WRITER keeps producing it. HIGH.
root_causes.RC-4: os.replace of a SQLite file is incompatible with SQLite's inode locking — and restore_snapshot takes a lock in NO domain. CRITICAL.
root_causes.RC-5: THE VERIFICATION LAYER ENCODES THE DEFECT — a green CI test asserts the data-loss outcome and calls it correct. CRITICAL.
root_causes.RC-6: `alive` conflates 'the process is running' with 'the work is succeeding'. MEDIUM.
root_causes.RC-7: The one IRREVERSIBLE learning actuator has the WEAKEST gate. MEDIUM. BLOCKED ON PD-3.
root_causes.RC-8: Post.error_reason carries four machine semantics; the fourth is a PERMANENT SUPPRESSION LATCH. MEDIUM.
root_causes.RC-9: Validators run at construction only; every mutation API bypasses them. LATENT (zero current reachability).
root_causes.RC-10: A mkdtemp with no owner. LOW.

new_findings.C4-F1a: bulk_send_to_review does not clear error_reason -> the stale reason latches reconcile.py:767 on pass ONE, so a reverted post goes silent IMMEDIATELY. Cycle 3 said 'one breadcrumb then silence'; on the reachable path there is ZERO breadcrumb, and the displayed reason LIES about the post's state. MEDIUM.
new_findings.C4-F2a: A restore racing a cascade-delete txn COMMITS into the orphaned inode -> the txn is NOT rolled back -> _drain_deferred_unlinks() PROCEEDS -> os.remove() DELETES REAL MEDIA while the restore brings the rows back. CRITICAL. Reached via the DOCUMENTED wipe-rollback procedure.
new_findings.C4-F5: Post.media_urls — NOT Render.path — is the durable pointer into the unmanaged mkdtemp dir (Studio oversize-retry, inside a committed txn). LOW: it SELF-HEALS via media_path_for_post's clip fallback.

superseded.C3-F5: RECLASSIFIED to UNREACHABLE. AST: Ledger.add_render has ZERO callers; Render(...) is constructed only in the two deserializers; crosspost.py:225 hardcodes render_id=None; LIVE LEDGER (read-only): 0 renders, 0/347 posts with a render_id. Cycle 3's EXP-10 hand-built a Render row — that proves the MECHANISM, not the REACHABILITY. Replaced by C4-F5.
superseded.C3-F2_root_cause: Cycle 3 blamed 'a sibling-parity divergence between the two status clients'. FALSE: both raise on >=300 identically (metrics.py:164-165 vs :516-517). The divergence is the REMOTE ENDPOINT SHAPE (Postiz: row absent from a 200-OK LIST page -> 'unknown'; Zernio: 404 on a PER-POST GET -> raise). The FINDING stands; the wrong cause implies the wrong (shallow) fix.
superseded.C3-F1_door: Cycle 3 named bulk_send_to_review on a needs_reconcile post. _REVIEW_REVERT_BLOCKED (actions.py:844-847) BLOCKS needs_reconcile. The real door is `failed`: reconcile polls, THE BACKEND REPORTS FAILED, the real sid is preserved (reconcile.py:735) -> revert -> approve -> strand. An ORDINARY operator workflow, which makes C3-F1 substantially MORE reachable than recorded.
superseded.C3-F4_C3-F5_coupling: Cycle 3's 'the fix for the leak would BREAK the pointer' is REFUTED BY EXECUTION (EXP-C4-4). media_path_for_post (compress.py:61-65) falls back to clip.path and the next shrink re-points media_urls at a fresh file. The cleanup is a SAFE, INDEPENDENT slice — no migration, no relocation, no atomicity requirement.

experiments_executed.EXP-C4-1: The FULL C3-F1 chain end-to-end through real code, via the CORRECTED `failed` door. Result: stranded in `submitting` at +100000h. Every assertion passed. Also surfaced C4-F1a.
experiments_executed.EXP-C4-2: The restore race re-proven (restorer NOT blocked; completed in 0.001s; writer's commit SUCCEEDED; row DESTROYED) — AND proved the existing test passes BECAUSE OF the loss (RC-5).
experiments_executed.EXP-C4-3: (A) C4-F2a: the restore race DELETES REAL MEDIA. (B) The proposed in-place fix VERIFIED: the restorer BLOCKED for 0.469s until the writer committed. (C) The corrupt-db fallback is REQUIRED (the in-place path raises DatabaseError; the os.replace path succeeds).
experiments_executed.EXP-C4-4: The claimed C3-F4 <-> C3-F5 coupling REFUTED. Render.path unreachable; media_urls is the real pointer; deleting the temp dir SELF-HEALS.

clusters_rejected.D_filesystem_and_ledger_durability: *** THIS CLUSTER DOES NOT EXIST. *** It is FOUR unrelated bugs that are merely all 'about files' (RC-4 / RC-10 / C3-F6 / C3-F7). None needs a 'unified storage contract'. The coupling that would have justified the abstraction is REFUTED by execution.
clusters_rejected.C_mutation_bypasses: The brief's framing is wrong. The seven writers of `queued` are NOT the problem — every non-approve_post writer is guarded on a source state of failed/error/submitting, so none can leave awaiting_approval. The property HOLDS; only the doc's claimed mechanism is false (INV-02, doc drift). The genuine root is RC-9, and it is LATENT.

sequence: S02 backend normalization at the read boundary
S03 claim refuses a post it will not POST
S06 revert clears the stale reason

parallelism.reframe_stream: VERIFIED PARALLEL-SAFE — zero file overlap AND zero state overlap (framing.py/keyframes.py have ZERO ledger references; clip.py's only three `Render` tokens are docstrings). One forward-looking hazard IF-1: a future reframe slice MINTING Render rows would reactivate the currently-unreachable C3-F5.

product_decisions_required.PD-1: Does the operator need an explicit 'republish' action? BLOCKS NOTHING — ship S03 anyway.
product_decisions_required.PD-2: Terminal for a real-token post that never resolves. Recommendation: the same age ladder (GAVE UP: is a LABEL, changes no state, cannot double-post).
product_decisions_required.PD-3: Is irreversible retirement at n=3 intentional? BLOCKS S10 ENTIRELY. No recommendation offered — not recoverable from code, and the guards that exist are evidence FOR intent.
product_decisions_required.PD-4: Malformed backend at load: normalize-and-skip, or raise? Recommendation: normalize case/whitespace; skip-and-flag an unknown name via the existing skipped_rows channel.

quality_gates_run.ast_mutation_census: RE-RUN. PostState writers by kind (literal / dynamic PostState(...) / setattr-dynamic-field / model_copy-literal / model_copy-dynamic-dict / ctor / enum keyword-default). PostState.error: 0 literal writers (still reserved). PostState.retired: 0 literal, but cli.py:395's dynamic PostState(args.status) DOES write it (confirms Cycle-2 SC-1).
quality_gates_run.exception_census: RE-RUN over the 15 files the proposed fixes touch: 194 handlers, 0 bare, 88 broad, 106 typed, 27 re-raise, 22 broad+silent.
quality_gates_run.sibling_parity: CONFIRMED: both status clients raise on >=300. The divergence is the ENDPOINT SHAPE, not the code.
quality_gates_run.render_census: AST: add_render callers = 0; Render(...) ctor sites = 2 (both deserializers); led.renders[...] stores = 4 (ALL model_copy UPDATES of an existing row). Live ledger: 0 renders / 0 of 347 posts with a render_id.
quality_gates_run.no_grep_only_claims: Every 'no writer' / 'zero caller' / 'never' / 'only' claim in the Cycle-4 deliverables is backed by an AST pass or full call-site tracing, per the Cycle-2 method note.
quality_gates_run.no_state_machine_change_creates_a_duplic: S04's GAVE UP: is a LABEL (no state change, not re-queueable). S03 strictly REMOVES a transition, adds none. Pinned by test_giveup_is_a_label_not_a_state_change.

cycle4.operational_constraint: OPS-001 STILL ENGAGED. The orchestration gate refused even a READ of the .orchestration/state/ACTIVE marker. FOUR consecutive cycles (1, 2, 3, 4) have now been executed SINGLE-THREADED. Disengaging the stale wave marker is an operator action from a human terminal; it is NOT a code defect, and it remains the single largest throughput constraint on this audit.

uninspected_modules_no_conclusion_depend.why_no_conclusion_depends_on_them: The AST censuses cover the FULL 127-module tree. None of these modules contains a ledger writer, a publish path, a lock primitive, or a status client. No root cause and no implementation slice depends on them.
